supporting_works.title: The pharmacopoeia universalis or, complete encyclopedia of the materia medica; contained in the pharmacopoeias of London, Edinburgh, and Dublin as well as of all those of Europe and America ; and of the dispensatories, formularies and chemical works ... supporting_works.creator: Smith, Daniel, 1790-

supporting_works.title: The reformed botanic and Indian physician : a complete guide to health / by Daniel Smith.

Culpeper's complete herbal ... To which are ... annexed his English physician enlarged, and Key to [Galen's Method of] physic ... to which is also added ... receipts selected from the author's Last legacy / Nicholas Culpeper.

Complete herbal ... To which is now first annexed his English physician enlarged, and Key to [Galen's Method of] physic ... to which is also added upwards of 50 choice receipts selected from the author's Last legacy. To his wife ... / [Nicholas Culpeper].

Complete herbal, to which is now added, upwards of one hundred additional herbs, with a display of their medicinal and occult qualities : physically applied to the cure of all disorders incident to mankind [etc.].

Culpeper's complete herbal ... To which are ... annexed his English physician enlarged, and Key to [Galen's Method of] physic ... to which is also added ... receipts selected from the author's Last legacy / [Nicholas Culpeper].

The complete herbal to which is now added upwards of one hundred additional herbs with a display of their medicinal and occult qualities physically applied to the cure of all disorders incident to mankind. To which are now first annexed the English physician enlarged, and Key to [Galen's Method of] physic with rules for compounding medicine according to the true system of nature forming a complete family dispensatory and natural system of physic / by Nicholas Culpeper ; to which is also added upwards of fifty choice receipts selected from the author's Last legacy. To his wife.

The complete herbal, to which is now added, upwards of one hundred additional herbs, with a display of their medicinal and occult qualities; physically applied to the cure of all disorders incident to mankind, To which is now annexed, the English physician enlarged, and Key to [Galen's Method of] physic ... to which is also added, upwards of fifty choice receipts selected form the author's Last legacy / [Nicholas Culpeper].

retrieval_text: Plantain (Plantago) is a medicinal herb profile in the Herbalisti historical corpus. It is represented across 157 works and 291 source-linked chunks. Recorded variants include Plantain (Plantago), Plantain. Common plant-part co-mentions include Root, Leaf, Seed, Flower, Bark, Gum. Common preparation co-mentions include Juice, Powder, Oil, Decoction, Tea, Poultice. Common condition co-mentions include Wounds or ulcers, Fever, Cold or catarrh, Cough, Consumption, Vomiting or nausea. Caution co-mentions include Dose caution, Toxicity watch, Child-use caution, Narcotic or sedative caution, Abortifacient warning, Bleeding caution. Representative source works include The pharmacopoeia universalis or, complete encyclopedia of the materia medica; contained in the pharmacopoeias of London, Edinburgh, and Dublin as well as of all those of Europe and America ; and of the dispensatories, formularies and chemical works ... With an appendix ... / by A. J. L. Jourdan : Edited by J. Rennie.; The reformed botanic and Indian physician : a complete guide to health; The reformed botanic and Indian physician : a complete guide to health / by Daniel Smith.; Culpeper's complete herbal ... To which are ... annexed his English physician enlarged, and Key to [Galen's Method of] physic ... to which is also added ... receipts selected from the author's Last legacy / Nicholas Culpeper.. This is a retrieval summary of historical co-mentions only, not a medical claim.
